urgency.message: 92 critical CVE(s) and 7 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
items.summary:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.

New research from Adversa AI, which is named the bypass GuardFall, found it works against ten of the eleven popular op

items.title: Lessons from the Underground: How to Combat Business Email Compromise
items.summary: Business Email Compromise is more than an email scam. It's a coordinated operation involving compromised accounts, financial research, and cash-out networks. Flare explores how underground forums reveal how BEC attacks are planned and executed. [...]

items.title: CVE-2026-42910 Windows Hotpatch Monitoring Service Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
items.summary: Updated an acknowledgement. This is an informational change only.

items.title: BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
items.summary:
The Microsoft Defender vulnerability CVE-2026-33825 was exploited in the wild as a zero-day before patches were released.
The post BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: An intelligence budget 'super user' job is now in the hands of Russ Vought
items.summary: Russell Vought, director of the White House Office of Management and Budget (OMB), assumed hands-on responsibility for overseeing the spending plans of intelligence agencies following the recent departure of Amaryllis Fox Kennedy, a senior intelligence official who simultaneously served in multiple

items.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
items.summary:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.

In many cases, the path in was visible just by watching what the app sent: a plaintext API key, a reusable token, or a backend server that accepted

items.title: How CISA BOD 26-04 redefines vulnerability management metrics for security leaders
items.summary: CISA’s BOD 26-04 changes how federal agencies patch and how security leaders must measure, justify, and communicate cyber risk to executives and boards.Key takeawaysBOD 26-04 requires agencies to make and defend risk-based vulnerability prioritization decisions, including decisions to defer vulnerab

items.title: Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks
items.summary:
Decades-old Bash shell tricks can bypass safeguards in most open source AI coding agents, potentially turning malicious repositories into supply chain attack vectors.
The post Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
items.summary: Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands.

items.title: Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million
items.summary:
Hackers accessed the insurance giant’s policyholder portal multiple times between June 15 and June 25.
The post Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Hacker Conversations: Chris Thompson, Former Head of IBM X-Force Red, Co-Founder of RemoteThreat
items.summary:
Chris Thompson's journey took him from hacking game controls as a teenager to founding IBM’s X-Force Red team.
The post Hacker Conversations: Chris Thompson, Former Head of IBM X-Force Red, Co-Founder of RemoteThreat appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ClickFix Now Cybercriminals' Favorite Malware Delivery Technique
items.summary: ReliaQuest report warns of a surge in ClickFix social engineering attacks against Windows and macOS users

items.title: Supreme Court Rules Constitutional Privacy Protections Apply to Cellphone Users’ Location History
items.summary:
The ruling was made in the case of a bank robber whose identity was discovered through a geofence warrant.
The post Supreme Court Rules Constitutional Privacy Protections Apply to Cellphone Users’ Location History appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
items.summary:
The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed. Threat actor activity was pre-planned, months out, across three sectors and at least ten languages.

Check Point E

items.title: Exploitation of Recent Oracle E-Business Suite Vulnerability Begins
items.summary:
The critical-severity defect allows unauthenticated attackers to take over the E-Business Suite’s Payments product.
The post Exploitation of Recent Oracle E-Business Suite Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
items.summary:
An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer.

The intrusion involves the exploitation of CVE-2026-48558 (CVSS score: 10.0), a critical authen

items.title: Insurance giant Aflac discloses data breach after subsidiary hack
items.summary: American insurance giant Aflac has disclosed a new data breach after attackers breached its Japan subsidiary's systems and stole personal and bank account information. [...]

items.title: Microsoft adds smarter bot protection to Teams meetings
items.summary: Microsoft has introduced a new Teams admin policy that allows organizers to prevent third-party bots from joining meetings without approval. [...]

items.title: Hackers Leverage Blockchain to Hit Japan's Hotels Through Booking.com Phishing
items.summary: A wave of phishing emails sent to Booking.com partner accommodations in Japan in May led to blockchain-hosted malware

items.title: The AI Token Costs That Can Break Cybersecurity
items.summary:
As cybersecurity platforms embrace agentic AI, organizations must balance detection performance against the escalating costs of token consumption, deployment architecture, and AI credits.
The post The AI Token Costs That Can Break Cybersecurity appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Kali Linux 2026.2 released with 9 new tools, NetHunter updates
items.summary: Kali Linux 2026.2, the second release of the year, is now available for download, featuring 9 new tools and numerous Kali NetHunter improvements. [...]

items.title: Blackfield ransomware asks Nidec Corporation for $2 million ransom
items.summary: The Blackfield ransomware gang is asking for a $2 million ransom from Nidec Corporation, a large Japanese manufacturer of electronic components for automotive and computing applications. [...]

items.title: UK Healthcare Sector Records Tenfold Increase in Cyber-Attacks
items.summary: SonicWall records 264,000 events in first five months of 2026 as UK hospitals come under siege

items.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
items.summary:
Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network.

An attacker within wireless range, with just a laptop and no prior connection, can crash the sharing service on a Mac or iPhone se

items.title: Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
items.summary:
Only a handful of the 100 organizations targeted in the PeopleSoft campaign have been confirmed.
The post Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs
items.summary: CISA confirmed on Monday that ransomware gangs are now exploiting a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation vulnerability, dubbed BlueHammer, that has previously been abused in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
items.summary:
Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six AI browsers and assistants into copying a user's credentials and sending them to an attacker.

items.title: Over 300 UK Firms Hit by Ransomware in a Year
items.summary: Report Fraud data reveals that more than half of 323 UK ransomware victims last year were SMEs

items.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
items.summary:
A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API.

The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8037, carries a CVSS score of 9.8 according to ZDI. A patch is available. If

items.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
items.summary:
A critical security flaw impacting Oracle E-Business Suite has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to Defused Cyber.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS score: 9.8), refers to an improper privilege management and authentication flaw in Oracle Payments that could be

items.title: NIST Enrichment Reductions Impact CVE Coverage, Accuracy
items.summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) scaled back the number of CVEs it selects for in-depth analysis, but the move has produced mixed results, according to researchers.

items.title: Meta Contractors Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
items.summary: Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.

items.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
items.summary: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.

items.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
items.summary: One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.

items.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
items.summary: Nissan is warning that it suffered a data breach affecting current and former employees after threat actors exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft vulnerability in data theft attacks previously linked to the ShinyHunters extortion group. [...]

items.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
items.summary: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) says the ShinyHunters extortion group stole only publicly available data, outdated logs, and configuration files after breaching its systems by exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in an Oracle PeopleSoft server. [...]

items.title: Can Clothes Make You Invisible to Facial Recognition?
items.summary: Does life feel Orwellian sometimes? One researcher has a solution for you: graphic tees that confuse the neural networks in surveillance cameras.

items.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
items.summary: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.

items.title: Justices rule that cellphone location histories are protected by the Fourth Amendment
items.summary: Police must get a warrant to request geofence data involving individual cellphones, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled in what represents a victory for privacy advocates.

items.title: US racks up about 400 wins over illegal World Cup streaming sites
items.summary: The World Cup’s organizing body, FIFA, helped identify hundreds of  domains taken down in an action organized by the U.S., along with the help of U.S. broadcaster NBC Universal and other entities.

items.title: US posts $10 million reward over Russian cyber campaign targeting Signal, WhatsApp
items.summary: Russia-linked hacking groups tracked as UNC5792 and UNC4221 have socially engineered their way into the messaging accounts of government officials.

items.title: OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access
items.summary: OpenAI is previewing its GPT-5.6 Sol model to a vetted few at the US government's request

items.title: Telegram-Based Millenium RAT Campaign Infects 60,000 Devices
items.summary: Group-IB says Millenium RAT, now rewritten in C++, has hit 62,289 devices in 160+ countries
